title: Wire Brave search + relevance into article collection pipeline
description:
Update ica/pipeline/article_collection.py to use BraveSearchClient and relevance assessment.

Current flow: GoogleSearchClient → deduplicate → parse_dates → upsert_articles
New flow: BraveSearchClient → deduplicate → LLM relevance assessment → upsert_articles (with excerpt + status + reason)

Changes to article_collection.py:
1. Replace GoogleSearchClient import with BraveSearchClient
2. Update keyword constants if needed (Brave may rank differently — keep same keywords initially)
3. After deduplication, call relevance_assessment.assess_articles() on all results
4. Merge relevance results back onto ArticleRecord (excerpt from search, status + reason from assessment)
5. Upsert all articles (both accepted and rejected) — relevance_status column distinguishes them
6. Update CollectionResult to include accepted_count and rejected_count
7. Update collect_articles() signature — accept BraveSearchClient instead of GoogleSearchClient

Changes to ica/pipeline/steps.py:
- Update _make_search() factory (or equivalent) to instantiate BraveSearchClient
- Wire relevance assessment LLM client into the collection flow

Logging:
- Log summary: 'Collected N articles, M accepted, K rejected by relevance filter'
close_reason: Wired BraveSearchClient and LLM relevance assessment into article collection pipeline. All 3704 tests pass.

title: Build end-to-end user test flow harness for full pipeline
description: Create a human-in-the-loop test harness that allows running the newsletter pipeline from start to finish, step-by-step, with real Slack and Google integrations, reproducible test data, and explicit continue-or-redo decisions at each checkpoint. The flow must be rerunnable, reviewable, and minimize manual setup by generating required test data automatically.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A single command starts the guided test flow from step 1 through final outputs. 2) Each step has generated/provisioned test data so users do not need to manually create fixtures. 3) Slack and Google Docs/Sheets interactions execute for test runs and are reviewable. 4) Users can choose continue, redo current step, or stop at each checkpoint. 5) The flow is rerunnable and can resume from persisted checkpoints. 6) Documentation covers setup, run, review, and cleanup.

title: Implement pipeline test-run state machine and checkpoints
description: Add a persisted test-run state model that tracks run_id, current step, step status, artifacts, operator choices, and timestamps. State must support continue, redo current step, restart from beginning, and resume after interruption.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Test run state persists across process restarts. 2) Step transitions are explicit and validated. 3) Redo re-executes only selected step with updated state history. 4) Resume restores pending step and context deterministically. 5) Unit tests cover transition rules and failure paths.

title: Add guided CLI command for end-to-end user test flow
description: Provide a command that runs the entire pipeline in guided mode, pausing at each step for operator decisions. The interface should show current step, artifact links, latest Slack prompt status, and options to continue, redo, or stop.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Single command launches guided run from step 1. 2) At every checkpoint user can choose continue, redo, or stop. 3) CLI output includes run_id and artifact references. 4) Keyboard flow is robust for interruption and resume. 5) Integration tests validate command behavior.
close_reason: Closed

title: Wire real Slack interactions into guided test mode
description: Ensure guided test runs execute real Slack send-and-wait interactions for each human review checkpoint, with correlation to test run state. Include timeout handling and replay-safe behavior for redo actions.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Slack prompts are sent for each human review step in guided mode. 2) Responses are captured and mapped to the correct run and step. 3) Redo generates a new interaction while retaining prior decision history. 4) Timeout and cancellation paths are handled and surfaced clearly. 5) Integration test coverage includes at least one full Slack decision loop.

title: Create GuidedSlackAdapter with run/step correlation and decision history
description: Wrap the existing Slack service with a GuidedSlackAdapter in ica/guided/slack_adapter.py. The adapter implements the same interface as SlackService but adds run_id and step_name metadata to every send_and_wait call. Injection point: add an optional slack_override parameter to run_guided() in runner.py. When provided, _build_step_registry() passes it through to steps instead of using _make_slack(). The adapter stores each interaction (message_ts, channel, response payload) in StepRecord.artifacts and appends to OperatorDecision history. Follow the existing pattern in runner.py where prompt_fn is already injectable for testing.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement timeout handling and cancellation for guided Slack prompts
description: Add configurable timeout for each Slack send_and_wait call in guided mode. When a timeout fires: (1) record the timeout in StepRecord, (2) transition the step to FAILED with a clear timeout error, (3) surface the timeout at the checkpoint prompt so the operator can redo or stop. Also handle KeyboardInterrupt and Slack API errors gracefully, mapping them to fail_step() calls with descriptive messages. Add a --slack-timeout CLI flag to the guided command (default: 300s).
close_reason: Closed

title: Make Slack redo replay-safe with new message generation per attempt
description: When the operator chooses REDO at a checkpoint, the next Slack interaction must send a new message rather than editing the previous one. Track the attempt number in the Slack message text (e.g., 'Step: Theme Generation (attempt 2)'). Retain all prior Slack decision payloads in the StepRecord so the full decision history is preserved across retries. Ensure no duplicate callback_ids by incorporating run_id + step + attempt into the callback identifier.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add integration test for full Slack send-and-wait decision loop
description: Write an integration test that exercises one complete Slack interaction cycle in guided mode: send prompt -> receive response -> record in state -> checkpoint -> operator continues. Use AsyncMock for the Slack client with realistic payloads. Verify: (1) message includes run_id/step metadata, (2) response is captured in artifacts, (3) redo sends a new message with incremented attempt, (4) timeout triggers fail_step, (5) decision history is complete after multiple interactions.

title: Design template storage model with version metadata and file-based persistence
description: Create a TemplateStore class in ica/guided/templates.py that manages HTML newsletter templates. Storage is file-based under a configurable directory (default: .guided-templates/). Each template version is a JSON file containing: template_html (the raw HTML), version (semver string), name, created_at, description, and a sha256 content hash for deduplication. The store supports multiple named templates each with multiple versions. Use a dataclass for TemplateRecord (consistent with the other guided/ module dataclasses like StepRecord and TestRunState) and keep Pydantic out of this layer.

title: Implement template CRUD operations (store, retrieve, list, select by version)

Handle edge cases: duplicate version (error), missing template (clear error with available alternatives), empty store. All operations are synchronous (file I/O, not async).

title: Bug: parse_doc_sections regex captures across newlines
description: In config_editor.py:22, _SECTION_RE uses [\w\s]* in the capture group, but \s matches newlines. With re.MULTILINE, the regex greedily matches content lines that contain only word chars and spaces (no punctuation), merging them into the section header name. Fix: replace \s with a literal space in the character class: r'^##\s+(\w[\w ]*)$'. Production is currently unaffected since real config values always contain punctuation (model names have /, descriptions have ., prompts are sentences), but any pure words-and-spaces value would silently break section parsing.

title: Consider multi-turn LLM approach for markdown validation retry loop
description: Currently each validation retry is a stateless single-turn LLM call — previous markdown and errors are injected into the prompt but the model has no awareness of what it tried before. If the same error persists across attempts, the LLM may repeat the same failing fix. Investigate whether switching to a multi-turn conversation (appending assistant/user message pairs across retries) would improve retry effectiveness. This would be a design change beyond the n8n original. Needs Kevin's input on whether this is worth the added complexity and token cost.
notes: Blocked pending Kevin's decision on whether to pursue this. The tradeoff: multi-turn gives the LLM retry memory (it can see what failed before), but increases token usage and diverges from the n8n original design.

- Merge all errors, feed back to LLM if invalid and under 3 attempts, force-accept after 3

User review: Share in Slack, collect approval/feedback. On approval, create Google Doc with markdown, share link. Store feedback in notes table.

Output: Updates PipelineContext with markdown_doc_id (Google Doc ID).

title: Make Google Sheets setup resilient: auto-create sheet and handle empty state
description:
The current curation flow assumes the Google Sheet already exists and has been shared with the service account. This is brittle — the user must manually create the sheet before the app can work.

Make the GoogleSheetsService resilient:

1. Auto-create spreadsheet if CURATED_ARTICLES_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID is empty or the sheet doesn't exist:
   - Use Google Sheets API to create a new spreadsheet
   - Persist the new sheet ID (update settings or store in DB/config)
   - Share it with the configured service account email

2. Handle empty sheet gracefully in the curation flow:
   - prepare_curation_data() should work fine with 0 articles from DB (already does)
   - The approval flow should present a clear message when the sheet is empty (no articles to approve)
   - Allow user to manually add articles to an empty sheet before clicking approve

3. Auto-create tabs (main + Rejected) if they don't exist:
   - When writing to a tab that doesn't exist, create it first
   - This supports the new 'Rejected' tab from ica-zqm without manual setup

This removes the manual Google Sheet setup step and lets users start from scratch — the app creates everything it needs on first run.
close_reason: Added create_spreadsheet, ensure_spreadsheet, ensure_tab to GoogleSheetsService with Drive API support. Updated steps.py to auto-create spreadsheet and tabs. 12 new tests.

title: Eliminate unawaited coroutine warnings in pytest runs
description: Regression analysis found recurring coroutine-leak warnings during otherwise passing tests. Repro command: docker exec ica-app-1 python -m pytest tests/. Focused runs of scheduler and article-collection suites show warnings including AsyncMockMixin._execute_mock_call was never awaited (scheduler tests) and Logging.async_success_handler was never awaited (article collection e2e tests). These warnings mask real async lifecycle defects and can hide true regressions in CI output.
acceptance_criteria: 1) docker exec ica-app-1 python -m pytest tests/ completes with zero unawaited coroutine warnings. 2) Scheduler tests no longer emit AsyncMockMixin._execute_mock_call was never awaited. 3) Article collection e2e tests no longer emit Logging.async_success_handler was never awaited from litellm. 4) Root causes are documented in issue notes with fixes in tests and/or production wiring as appropriate.
notes: Root causes: (1) Scheduler tests patched asyncio.create_task as plain MagicMock, so coroutines from AsyncMock._run_pipeline were never consumed — leaked coroutines triggered RuntimeWarning on GC. Fix: added _close_coro helper that closes the coroutine argument. This also fixed the GC-leaked warnings in brave_search and other scheduler tests. (2) litellm LoggingWorker creates async_success_handler coroutine during cleanup that cannot be awaited outside its event loop — third-party library bug. Fix: added filterwarnings in pyproject.toml to suppress.
close_reason: Fixed: scheduler tests now close coroutines via _close_coro helper; litellm warning suppressed via pytest filterwarnings. Full suite: 3862 passed, 0 warnings.

title: Create LLM relevance assessment config and module
description:
Add an LLM-based relevance screening step that evaluates search results before DB storage.

LLM Config (new file: ica/llm_configs/relevance-assessment-llm.json):
- Schema: ica-llm-config/v1
- processName: relevance-assessment
- Model: google/gemini-2.5-flash (fast + cheap — this runs per-article on every collection cycle)
- Instruction prompt should:
  - Accept article title and excerpt (search snippet)
  - Evaluate fit for the IS2 newsletter audience: solopreneurs and SMB professionals interested in AI for business
  - Return structured output: { "decision": "accept" | "reject", "reason": "<~20 words>" }
  - Criteria: practical AI relevance, business applicability, newsworthiness, not overly academic/niche

LLM Purpose (ica/config/llm_config.py):
- Add RELEVANCE_ASSESSMENT = 'llm_relevance_assessment_model' to LLMPurpose enum
- Add process name mapping in PURPOSE_TO_PROCESS

Pipeline module (new file: ica/pipeline/relevance_assessment.py):
- RelevanceResult dataclass: url, decision (accept/reject), reason (str)
- assess_article(title, excerpt, llm_client) → RelevanceResult
- assess_articles(search_results, llm_client) → list[RelevanceResult] — runs assessments (can be sequential since Gemini Flash is fast and cheap)
- Parse LLM response JSON, handle malformed responses gracefully (default to accept if parsing fails)

Prompt module (new file: ica/prompts/relevance_assessment.py):
- build_relevance_prompt(title, excerpt) → str — injects article data into instruction template
close_reason: Closed
